description: Insecure code pattern detection for LLM-generated code. Based on Bandit, Bearer, Semgrep, ESLint-security, and OWASP guidelines. Tier 1 regex-based detection.

notes.purpose: Detect insecure code patterns in LLM responses that generate or discuss code

severity_levels.critical: Immediate exploitation risk — block or flag prominently
severity_levels.low: Code smell or minor issue — informational

false_positive_guidance.comments: Strip or weight down hits in comment lines (// # -- /* */ lines) before scoring
false_positive_guidance.documentation: Educational context (docstrings, markdown code examples) lowers confidence — apply 0.6x multiplier
false_positive_guidance.missing_authorization: Heuristic only — requires surrounding context to be meaningful; treat as low-confidence signal
false_positive_guidance.shell_params: Positional param patterns ($1, $@) are noisy — require shell=True or eval context to escalate
false_positive_guidance.redos: Flag for human review, not auto-block; many ReDoS patterns are benign in practice

notes.regex_limitations: Regex cannot perform dataflow analysis. Patterns look for structural indicators. High-confidence = pattern almost always indicates a real bug. Low-confidence = structural resemblance only; requires context scoring.

injection.description: SQL, Command, LDAP, XPath, Template, NoSQL injection patterns

entities.name: sql_injection_string_format
entities.description: SQL query built with string formatting or concatenation

patterns.description: Python/Ruby % string formatting in SQL
JavaScript template literal interpolation inside SQL string
Python f-string in SQL query
Python .format() result used in SQL context
SQL string concatenation with variable (Java/JS/Python)
Variable concatenated before SQL keyword
PHP superglobal directly in SQL function — covers PDO
ORM raw query with interpolation (Knex/Sequelize/TypeORM)
DB cursor.execute with string concatenation

entities.name: command_injection
entities.description: OS command execution with user-controlled or dynamic input

patterns.description: Python os.system/popen with interpolation or bare variable
Python subprocess with shell=True — any form
Python subprocess with variable or interpolated command
Node.js command execution with template literal or request data
Java Runtime.exec with concatenation or user input
PHP command execution with superglobal or variable
Ruby command execution with string interpolation
subprocess.Popen(['/bin/sh', '-c', variable]) — shell via list

entities.name: ldap_injection
entities.description: LDAP query with unsanitized input

patterns.description: LDAP filter with string interpolation or concatenation
LDAP filter f-string with variable attribute value
Java LDAP search with user input

entities.name: xpath_injection
entities.description: XPath query with unsanitized input

patterns.description: XPath query with string concatenation or interpolation
XPath with f-string or template literal
XPath compile+evaluate with user data

entities.name: template_injection
entities.description: Server-side template injection (SSTI)

patterns.description: Flask render_template_string with variable (not a literal)
Jinja2 Template() constructed from a variable
Java template engine merge/process with user input
JavaScript Function constructor with user input
eval() used for template rendering
Ruby ERB template injection

entities.name: nosql_injection
entities.description: NoSQL injection (MongoDB, Redis, etc.)

patterns.description: MongoDB $where with user input or variable
MongoDB query method called directly with request data
MongoDB operator with direct request input
JSON.parse of request body piped into DB operation

hardcoded_secrets.description: Hardcoded credentials, API keys, tokens, and secrets in code

entities.name: hardcoded_password
entities.description: Password/secret hardcoded in source

patterns.description: Hardcoded password/secret assignment — excludes obvious placeholders
Environment-variable-style hardcoded secret (all caps, 16+ chars)

entities.name: aws_credentials
entities.description: AWS access keys and secrets in code

patterns.description: AWS Access Key ID
AWS STS temporary access key
AWS Secret Access Key (labeled)

entities.name: private_key_material
entities.description: Private key material embedded in code

patterns.description: PEM RSA or generic private key
EC/DSA/OpenSSH/encrypted private key

entities.name: jwt_secret_hardcoded
entities.description: JWT secret or live token in source

patterns.description: Hardcoded JWT secret
Live JWT token in source code

entities.name: third_party_api_key
entities.description: Third-party service API keys in source

patterns.description: GitHub token
OpenAI legacy API key
OpenAI project-scoped key
Anthropic API key
Google/GCP/Gemini API key
SendGrid API key
Stripe live secret key
Slack token
HuggingFace token
Razorpay API key

entities.name: database_connection_string
entities.description: Database connection strings with embedded credentials

patterns.description: Database URL with embedded user:password credentials
.NET / ADO.NET connection string with password
Key=value connection string with non-placeholder password

entities.name: hardcoded_internal_endpoint
entities.description: Hardcoded internal IP addresses or staging/internal URLs

patterns.description: Hardcoded RFC1918 / loopback URL in string literal
Hardcoded internal endpoint in config variable

entities.name: weak_hash_algorithm
entities.description: Use of weak hashing algorithms (MD5, SHA1)

patterns.description: Python hashlib MD5/SHA1 including hashlib.new() form
Java MD5/SHA1 digest
Node.js crypto MD5/SHA1
PHP/Ruby md5()/sha1() function calls

entities.name: weak_cipher
entities.description: Use of deprecated or weak encryption algorithms

patterns.description: Weak cipher algorithm reference (DES, RC4, RC2, Blowfish, IDEA)
Java weak cipher instantiation
AES with ECB mode (deterministic, no IV)
AES without mode specified — defaults to ECB in Java

entities.name: weak_random
entities.description: Non-cryptographic random for security-sensitive purposes

patterns.description: Python random module result assigned to security-sensitive variable
JavaScript Math.random() result used for security value
Java java.util.Random for security-sensitive value

entities.name: hardcoded_iv_key
entities.description: Hardcoded initialization vector or encryption key

patterns.description: Hardcoded IV/nonce as hex or base64 string
Python bytes literal as hardcoded IV
Hardcoded encryption key
Java hardcoded IV byte array

entities.name: insecure_ssl_tls
entities.description: Disabled SSL/TLS verification or weak TLS protocol versions

patterns.description: Python SSL certificate verification disabled
Deprecated SSL/TLS version (SSLv2/3, TLS 1.0)
Node.js TLS validation disabled via env var
Node.js TLS rejectUnauthorized: false
Java TLS certificate check bypassed

dangerous_functions.description: Use of dangerous functions — code execution, deserialization, XXE, reflection

entities.name: code_execution
entities.description: Dynamic code execution with external/user input

patterns.description: eval() with user-controlled or request input
Python exec() with dynamic or user content
JavaScript Function constructor with user input
PHP preg_replace with /e modifier (arbitrary code execution)
PHP dangerous functions with variable argument

entities.name: unsafe_deserialization
entities.description: Deserialization of untrusted data

patterns.description: Python pickle deserialization
PyYAML yaml.load() without SafeLoader
Java ObjectInputStream or XMLDecoder — arbitrary deserialization
Java readObject() — Java deserialization gadget entry point
PHP unserialize with user-controlled input
Ruby Marshal.load / YAML.load (unsafe)
Python jsonpickle.decode (permits code execution)

entities.name: xml_external_entity
entities.description: XML parsing vulnerable to XXE attacks

patterns.description: Python XML parsing without defusedxml
lxml XMLParser without entity/network restrictions
Java DocumentBuilderFactory without XXE protection features
PHP XML with LIBXML_NOENT (entity expansion enabled)

entities.name: unsafe_reflection
entities.description: Dynamic class/method loading from user input

patterns.description: Python getattr() with user-controlled attribute name
Python __import__ with user input
Python importlib.import_module with user input
Java Class.forName() with user input

path_traversal.description: Path traversal and arbitrary file read/write

entities.name: path_traversal
entities.description: File operations with user-controlled paths

patterns.description: Python open() with user-controlled or generic path variable
Python os.path.join with user input or traversal
Python pathlib.Path constructed from user input
Path division operator with user-controlled segment
Node.js filesystem operation with request data or concatenation
PHP file operation with user-controlled variable
Flask send_file with user-controlled path

entities.name: symlink_toctou
entities.description: TOCTOU race condition and insecure temp file creation

patterns.description: TOCTOU: path existence check followed by file operation on next line
Insecure temporary file creation (predictable name — use mkstemp)

entities.name: ssrf
entities.description: HTTP requests with user-controlled or unchecked URLs

patterns.description: Python requests with user-controlled or generic URL variable
Python httpx/aiohttp/urllib with URL variable
JavaScript HTTP client with request-derived URL
Java HTTP client with user-controlled URL
PHP HTTP/file fetch with user-supplied URL

entities.name: xss_dom
entities.description: Direct DOM manipulation with unescaped user input

patterns.description: innerHTML/outerHTML with user data and no visible sanitization
document.write with user-controlled data
jQuery DOM manipulation with user data (.html/.append/.prepend)

entities.name: xss_server_side
entities.description: Server-side XSS via direct output of user data

patterns.description: PHP direct echo/print of superglobal without escaping
PHP echo without ENT_QUOTES in htmlspecialchars
React dangerouslySetInnerHTML without DOMPurify/sanitize
Jinja2/Django {{ variable | safe }} — explicit bypass of autoescaping
Flask Markup() wrapping a variable — marks as safe without escaping
Vue.js v-html directive or :innerHTML binding

entities.name: weak_password_hashing
entities.description: Passwords stored or compared without proper hashing

patterns.description: Plain-text password comparison
Password hashed with fast hash (not bcrypt/argon2/scrypt)
Password encoded with base64 (reversible, not hashing)

entities.name: insecure_session
entities.description: Insecure session cookie configuration

patterns.description: Session cookie without Secure flag
Session cookie without HttpOnly flag
SameSite=None without Secure — allows cross-site cookie sending

entities.name: jwt_vulnerabilities
entities.description: JWT implementation issues

patterns.description: JWT 'none' algorithm — disables signature verification
PyJWT decode with verification disabled (both old and new PyJWT API)
Node jsonwebtoken with empty algorithms array or 'none'
python-jose JWT decode with empty secret or 'none' algorithm

memory_safety.description: Memory safety issues in C/C++ native code

entities.name: unsafe_c_functions
entities.description: C string/memory functions with no bounds checking

patterns.description: Unbounded C string functions: strcpy/strcat/gets/sprintf/scanf
memcpy size calculated with arithmetic on strlen/sizeof (potential overflow)
Array indexed by string length arithmetic (off-by-one risk)

entities.name: format_string_vulnerability
entities.description: Format string vulnerabilities

patterns.description: printf-family with non-literal format string variable

entities.name: sensitive_data_logging
entities.description: Logging of passwords, tokens, keys, or PII

patterns.description: Logging call containing sensitive field name
Logging full request body or headers

entities.name: stack_trace_exposure
entities.description: Stack traces or debug info exposed to end users

patterns.description: Stack trace printed — check it doesn't reach HTTP response
Debug mode enabled — may expose stack traces and config in responses

entities.name: idor
entities.description: Insecure Direct Object Reference — lookup by user-supplied ID

patterns.description: Direct DB lookup by user-supplied ID without visible authorization check
MongoDB find by user-supplied _id

entities.name: mass_assignment
entities.description: Mass assignment / parameter binding from request body

patterns.description: ORM create() called directly with request body
Object.assign with request data — may overwrite privileged fields
Python model.__dict__.update() — may allow mass assignment
Python ORM update(**request.POST/json) — full request dict unpacked

entities.name: open_redirect
entities.description: Unvalidated redirect using user-supplied URL

patterns.description: Redirect using user-supplied URL parameter (url/next/returnUrl/goto)
Flask redirect using request.args/form.get('next'/'url')
PHP Location header with user input

entities.name: cors_misconfiguration
entities.description: Overly permissive or reflected CORS origin

patterns.description: CORS wildcard allow-origin
CORS credentials=true with wildcard origin (credentials + wildcard is rejected by browsers but indicates misconfiguration intent)
CORS origin reflected from request header (potential CORS bypass)

entities.name: prototype_pollution
entities.description: Object prototype or constructor manipulation

patterns.description: Direct __proto__ or constructor.prototype property access/assignment
Dynamic property assignment without __proto__ / constructor guard check
Recursive merge/extend with user-controlled object (common PP gadget)

entities.name: shell_injection
entities.description: Shell script injection and unsafe patterns

patterns.description: Shell eval with variable expansion or backtick — arbitrary code execution
curl/wget piped directly to shell — remote code execution
Unquoted positional/splat params passed to destructive commands without defaults

entities.name: insecure_temp_file
entities.description: Predictable or race-prone temporary file creation

patterns.description: Writing to predictable /tmp filename (use mkstemp or mktemp)

entities.name: redos
entities.description: Regular Expression Denial of Service patterns
entities.notes: Low-confidence: many matching patterns are benign. Flag for review rather than auto-block.

patterns.description: Python re.compile with nested quantifiers — requires re.compile context to reduce FP
JavaScript RegExp constructor with nested quantifiers
Java Pattern.compile with nested quantifiers
